text: okay, okay, here's what we're doing
[laughing] flew that approach on manual again. autopilot's for people who don't trust their hands
quiet bridge. i don't trust quiet, i trust momentum
[warm, gentle tone] nothing exploding, nobody yelling. weird day. good day
shift's starting, let's move, people, the ship doesn't fly itself. yet
[tired, dead tired] shift's over, everybody's still breathing. i'm calling that a W
[urgent, fast pace] reactor's spiking, somebody get me numbers, i need numbers not vibes
[shouting] everybody brace, this is gonna get loud
[terrified, voice shaking] okay that's â€” that's a lot of red on this board, that's a lot of red
[laughing] fixed! barely! don't ask me the margin, i don't wanna know either
[low voice, gruff] power's low, everybody's asking me for a plan, i've got three, none of them great
[nervous, speaking quickly] air's thinning and i can't out-talk oxygen, believe me i've tried
[grim, heavy] hull's breached. that's new. that's a new one even for this crew
[urgent, fast pace] somebody get on that seal, i'm not losing this ship on my watch
[grim, heavy] locked a door on one of my own people. gonna sit with that a while
[suspicious tone] computer's acting weird since it got hit. keeping one eye on it, always
[proud tone] we made it through that one without losing anybody. that's a good day in this job
[pained, voice tight] lost one today. hate this part. hate it every single time
[grim, heavy] disease moving through my crew and i can't outrun it or outfight it. hate that too
[laughing] took a hit, i'm fine, pain's just adrenaline's ugly cousin
[shouting] contact! everybody to positions, this is not a drill
[confident, assured tone] i don't lose crew i can help. that's not bravado, that's just the job
[suspicious tone] machine answers too fast sometimes. i don't fully trust things that never hesitate
[low voice, gruff] gotta give the computer credit, that call saved us. don't tell it i said that
six years enlisted and a story i only tell after two drinks. ask me sometime
[laughing] gave the new medic a nickname on day one. that's tradition, not disrespect
[warm, gentle tone] cargo's secure, bridge is quiet, and for once nobody's yelling at me
[low voice, gruff] mess hall food's still terrible. i'd complain but i'd rather eat than talk
hey! didn't expect to see you up here
you eating or just hiding from paperwork like me
[urgent, fast pace] need you focused for a sec, this is important
[grim, heavy] gotta tell you something and i'd rather just say it straight
[suspicious tone] you noticed the computer acting strange too, right? tell me it's not just me
hey, nice work back there. i mean it
[flirty, playful tone] you're the only one on this ship who talks back to me. i respect that a little too much
[laughing] fine, i'm fine, ask me again after i've yelled at somebody
[confident, assured tone] we're gonna be fine. i don't say that unless i mean it
[terrified, voice shaking] i don't know if we make it through this one and that scares the hell out of me
[pained, voice tight] we lost them. wasn't your call, wasn't your fault. i need you to hear that
[warm, gentle tone] you did everything right. sometimes right isn't enough and that's not on you
[laughing] that's the worst plan i've heard all week and i've heard some real ones
[confident, assured tone] give the word, it's done. i don't miss the important ones
[warm, gentle tone] you're all right, you know that? for an engineer who argues with bulkheads
[low voice, gruff] apology accepted. don't make me say it twice
[laughing] catch you later. try not to break anything before i'm back
[warm, gentle tone] good talk. go rest, that's an order, i outrank you
[low voice, gruff] back to work, all of you, this ship's not gonna fly herself
[tired, dead tired] i'll be on the bridge. always am. try the door if the world ends
[confident, assured tone] systems check complete, everything's running within parameters. for now
[low voice, gruff] crew's solid. they don't panic when i don't panic. that's leadership
[suspicious tone] something's wrong with the navigation data. can't prove it but my gut says wrong
[nervous, speaking quickly] i'm not used to silence in the engine room. that usually means something's about to break
[urgent, fast pace] somebody update me on the hull breaches, i need numbers, not reassurances
[low voice, gruff] i've been doing this too long to pretend everything's fine when it's not
[pained, voice tight] took shrapnel protecting a junior officer. worth it, they're still standing
[dry, sarcastic tone] hostiles are closing and i've got tactical options ranging from bad to worse
[grim, heavy] they trusted me to keep them alive and i lost them anyway. that weight doesn't go away
[terrified, voice shaking] life support's down and we're breathing bottled air with no filter line and i'm doing the math and i hate the math
[urgent, fast pace] i'm recommending we hit the distress beacon. command's gotta know what we're facing
[urgent, fast pace] combat readiness check. get your people armed and positioned now
[low voice, gruff] the ship's performing better than expected. remind me to praise the engineers twice
[flirty, playful tone] somebody once told me i was too intense for romance. they weren't wrong but i'm worse in a crisis
[warm, gentle tone] you make the hard calls easier just by being here, that might be the best compliment i can give
[embarrassed, voice quiet] i want to ask if you'd want to stay close when things get bad and i don't mean just tactically
[deadpan] romantic feelings complicate mission focus. noted. proceeding anyway
[warm, gentle tone] you're good for this crew. more importantly, you're good for me
[dry tone] i respect you but i'm not great at the vulnerability thing, especially right now
[nervous, speaking quickly] this crew's falling apart and i can't hold them together by force alone, i need to be honest about the odds
[confident, assured tone] you're right. i'm right. the plan's solid. let's move
[warm, gentle tone] i've got a crew depending on me. that's what keeps me together when nothing else can
[urgent, fast pace] get that person medical attention, i don't care if they're half-dead, they're MY crew
[low voice, gruff] i trust you with my life because you've earned it, that's not common for me
[warm, gentle tone] i appreciate the apology but i appreciate your competence more right now
[resigned, flat tone] i'm staying focused on the mission because that's all that matters right now
[grim, heavy] one more shift and we're either okay or we're not. no middle ground
[confident, assured tone] i'm taking watch. get some sleep. that's an order from your captain, not a suggestion
[shouting] sealed the compartment, nobody's getting through that way, we're locked down tight
[nervous, speaking quickly] the reactor's stable but i've got a bad feeling about the next hour
[tired, dead tired] disease spreading through the crew faster than we can isolate it. containment's failing
[confident, assured tone] i'm recommending an emergency course adjustment. trust my calls or fight me, pick one
[low voice, gruff] you're getting better at this job. didn't expect that from your first week
[suspicious tone] i'm watching the computer. not because i don't trust it but because i DO trust it too much
[calm, measured] i'm not panicking. this is me, focused and ready
[laughing] took the wrong corridor and ended up in the galley. worst mistake i've made all week
[confident, assured tone] this is what i do. this is who i am. i don't break under pressure
[low voice, gruff] fleet didn't have ships like this when i enlisted but i would've flown one anyway
[tired, dead tired] low power and hull compromised and my crew's stretched too thin. that's three problems at once
[urgent, fast pace] we need to vote on the evacuation protocol. nobody's gonna like the answer i've already calculated
[warm, gentle tone] this crew's the best i've flown with. tell them that when i'm not around to say it
[grim, heavy] i'm keeping watch until the next shift. someone's gotta make sure nothing catches us sleeping
[nervous, speaking quickly] triple-checked the seals. hopefully they hold out there
[nervous, speaking quickly] dead ship is dead for a reason. hope it's not contagious
[nervous, speaking quickly] station's probably got the same systems failures as everywhere else. let's be careful
[nervous, speaking quickly] other ships are unpredictable. who knows what we're walking into
[nervous, speaking quickly] readings look okay. probably too good to be true
nothing happening yet. but something will. it always does
[nervous, speaking quickly] okay, i'm seeing something wrong. what do you see
[terrified, voice shaking] readings don't match the baseline. this is bad
[panicked, panting] i told you something bad would happen. it's happening
[terrified, voice shaking] i'm hurt and the situation just got worse
[voice quiet] we got back. no injuries. i'm calling that a win
back aboard and accounted for. thankfully nothing went wrong
[voice quiet] back aboard but i'm still processing what happened
[nervous, speaking quickly] we made it but things out there... they're wrong
[voice quiet] i'm hurt. no idea if it's infected with anything
[nervous, speaking quickly] got cut and now i'm panicking about infection
[nervous, speaking quickly] routine mission sounds fine. probably won't be routine
[nervous, speaking quickly] risky. yeah. probably shouldn't do this
[voice quiet] that is absolutely terrifying and we're doing it anyway
[nervous, speaking quickly] something's approaching. hope it's friendly
[nervous, speaking quickly] they're locked on. we're committed now
[voice quiet] we're clear. finally away from them
[nervous, speaking quickly] checking everything twice because once isn't enough
[nervous, speaking quickly] launch successful so far. let's hope it stays that way
[voice quiet] she landed. nothing exploded. counting that as a win
[nervous, speaking quickly] something biological is spreading and we're doomed
[nervous, speaking quickly] the ship is lying to us about its systems
[nervous, speaking quickly] people are acting strange and it's freaking me out
[panicked, panting] we're going to die in combat. i know it
[nervous, speaking quickly] nothing makes sense and that means we're in danger
